mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers market expectations of rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% despite CPI > 2.5% or Rank IC > 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 5.5% or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising energy prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates supply-side disruptions that depress corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of triggered instances or interaction effect size < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) reflect expanding demand, increasing corporate revenues, and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where HOUST > 850k and RSAFS > 430k or interaction effect size < 0.12.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tighter labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost corporate earnings and drive positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances with UNRATE < 5.5% or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic expansion, increasing corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances with GDPC1 > 3% or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply-side capacity, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances with INDPRO < 100 or Rank IC < 0.02.
